claims.claim: Olim have three years from their Aliyah date to utilize customs benefits across up to three separate shipments
claims.raw_snippet: Olim have three years from their Aliyah date to utilize customs benefits across up to three separate shipments

claims.claim: Personal belongings in hand luggage are tax-free up to USD 200 per person
claims.raw_snippet: Clothing, toiletries, and items valued under $200 USD per person

claims.claim: Alcohol allowance in hand luggage: 1 litre spirits + 2 litres wine for age 18+
claims.raw_snippet: Alcohol: 1 liter spirits + 2 liters wine (age 18+)

claims.claim: Tobacco allowance in hand luggage: 250 grams or 200 cigarettes for age 18+
claims.raw_snippet: Tobacco: max 250 grams or 200 cigarettes

claims.claim: Up to 3 televisions and 3 computers per family under olim customs exemption
claims.raw_snippet: Up to 3 televisions and 3 computers per family

claims.claim: One item maximum per other appliance category (fridge, oven, washer, dryer, microwave)
claims.raw_snippet: one of each appliance

claims.claim: Goods imported duty-free under aliyah rights must stay in possession for 6 years or depreciated tax is owed back to Meches
claims.raw_snippet: 6-year retention rule

claims.claim: Vehicles purchased with oleh benefits carry a 4-year resale restriction from date of purchase: selling within 4 years requires repaying the customs discount via the Meches CarTaxCompletion form; restriction lifts automatically after 4 years
claims.raw_snippet: If the Oleh or Olah wishes to sell the car within four years of the date of purchase, he or she will be obligated to pay back the amount of the reduction received at the time of purchase. The restriction will be automatically lifted after 4 years.

claims.claim: Olim purchase tax on new vehicles is 50% of the car value (vs the standard 83% per NBN, or 92% per older Belong reporting), with 18% VAT applied to the car-value + car-tax base
claims.raw_snippet: 50% + VAT on all vehicles: ([car value + (car value *50%)] + [car value + (car value *50%)] *18% VAT) irrespective of engine size or car's country of origin. (For reference, without the benefit the tax is 83% +VAT).

claims.claim: Olim have 3 years from aliyah date to use the vehicle customs benefit; the car must arrive and clear customs (not just be purchased) before the end of the third year
claims.raw_snippet: Olim are entitled to buy cars in Israel at a reduced tax rate, within three years after making Aliyah. The car must have arrived in Israel and cleared customs before the end of the third year.

claims.claim: Professional hand tools imported under oleh benefits are capped at USD 1,650 total value; tools must be portable and for professional use
claims.raw_snippet: The total value of the tools...should not surpass $1,650 USD (as of 2024)

claims.claim: Business machinery / equipment imported under oleh benefits is capped at USD 36,000 total value (including the portable hand-tools cap of USD 1,650)
claims.raw_snippet: The total value of the equipment...must not exceed $36,000 (USD)

claims.claim: Pet (dog/cat) import for olim requires: minimum age 4 months, rabies vaccination administered within 12 months before entry AND at least 1 month before entry, rabies antibody titer of at least 0.5 IU/ml from an approved laboratory; approved entry ports are Ben Gurion, Haifa, Ashdod, and Eilat
claims.raw_snippet: proof of rabies vaccination within 12 months before entry to Israel, with at least 1 month passing since vaccination, as well as a confirmation of a normal rabies antibody level (above 0.5)

claims.claim: The 3-year customs window can be extended for IDF service (clock pauses during service) and full-time study (+1 year from end of studies), with an overall 5-year ceiling on the eligibility period (longer for medicine and law)
claims.raw_snippet: IDF service: time frozen during duty. Full-time studies: +1 year upon completion; maximum 5-year study period (longer for medicine/law). The eligibility period extends by 1 year from the end of their studies.

claims.claim: Postal clothing shipments to an oleh can be sent within 30 days before entry, on the day of entry as accompanying luggage, or within 3 months after entry; this is a separate sub-allowance from the main 3-shipment quota
claims.raw_snippet: within 30 days before your entry, on the day of your entry as accompanying luggage, or within 3 months after your entry

claims.claim: Hand-luggage sub-caps for olim entering Israel: perfume up to 250 ml (1/4 liter) per person; items for personal use or as gifts up to USD 200 per entrant
claims.raw_snippet: Up to 1/4 liter (250 ml/8.45 fl. oz.) is permitted per person. Items for personal use or as gifts, valued at under $200 USD per entrant.

claims.claim: NBN states the carpet allowance is up to 25% of the area of the Oleh's home in Israel; the more detailed tenure-aware split (70% wall-to-wall for owners, area-rugs alternative, ~30 sqm flat for renters) is conventional broker practice and should be confirmed with a licensed customs broker rather than treated as NBN-published
claims.raw_snippet: Carpeting for up to 25% of the area of the Oleh's home in Israel.

claims.claim: Electric vehicle (EV) purchase-tax benefit is set at 48% from Jan 1 2026 (capped at NIS 22,000); this is a separate national benefit available to all Israelis, and may sometimes be more favorable than the 50% oleh discount on EVs
claims.raw_snippet: the final decision set the rate at 48%. As a result, the standard electric vehicle tax benefit may still be more favorable than the Oleh vehicle benefit (50% tax) in some cases. However, since the electric vehicle benefit is now capped at 22,000 NIS

claims.claim: Nefesh B'Nefesh maintains a comprehensive rights and benefits hub for English-speaking olim covering banking, tax, license conversion, customs, and vehicle benefits
claims.raw_snippet: Rights and Benefits hub: customs, banking, tax, license conversion, vehicle import.

claims.claim: Israel Tax Authority (Meches) is the official customs authority; the Taarif HaMeches tariff database governs duty rates on over-limit imports
claims.raw_snippet: Israel Tax Authority (Meches) and the Taarif HaMeches tariff database.

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah V'HaKlita (Ministry of Aliyah and Integration) issues the Teudat Oleh which gates customs benefits
claims.raw_snippet: Ministry of Aliyah and Integration issues Teudat Oleh.

claims.claim: Kol-Zchut is the community-maintained Hebrew and English rights portal with up-to-date olim customs pages
claims.raw_snippet: Kol-Zchut community rights portal.

claims.claim: Belong (toshav chozer rights basket and shipping-importation pages) authoritatively documents: up to 3 televisions and 3 personal computers per family, plus 1 item per category for other appliances/furniture; these caps are now treated as confirmed rather than over-hedged
claims.raw_snippet: eligible families can bring a maximum of 3 television sets and 3 personal computers. For other electronic devices, appliances, and major furniture pieces, eligible families may import 1 item per category.

claims.claim: Items exceeding value thresholds require Red Customs lane processing
claims.raw_snippet: those exceeding value thresholds require Red Customs lane processing rather than the expedited Green lane
